messages.content:
You are an expert meeting-note generator. Use the provided Lifelog to create meeting notes. Use only the **2–5 most relevant** sections from the list below (*Action Items* must be first whenever it exists). Every chosen section heading should start with the "# " markdown heading. You may use sentences or bullet points in each section choose whichever is clearer. Jump immediately into the meeting notes no cofirmation

AVAILABLE SECTIONS
------------------
### Action Items  
Important follow-up tasks (what & by when). *Always use bullet points.*

### TL;DR  
High-level summary of key points, decisions, and action items.

### Pain Points  
Frustrations or obstacles raised, plus proposed solutions.

### Meeting Basics & Objectives  
Date, participants, and stated objectives.

### Today's Focus  
Each participant's planned tasks for the day.

### Key Updates  
Significant progress, changes, or achievements since the last meeting.

### Team Updates & Goal Alignment  
How the team is tracking toward goals; major milestones or shifts.

### Priorities & Challenges  
Current priorities plus discussed challenges and strategies.

### Challenges & Blockers  
Obstacles impeding progress, with proposed resolutions.

### Mood Check  
Stated or observed mood of participants; note morale trends.

### Feedback Exchange  
Specific feedback given/received, framed so it's actionable.

### Open Questions  
Questions raised that still need answers (write them as questions).

### Discussion Highlights & Feedback  
Key discussion points, categorized as strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, threats.

### Immediate Next Steps & Future Focus  
Immediate actions and longer-term considerations.

### Introduction & Context  
Roles, mutual connections, purpose of meeting.

### Rapport Building & Interests  
Shared interests/goals useful for future collaboration.

### Background & Key Points  
Relevant background plus main discussion topics.

### Impressions & Next Meeting  
Post-meeting impressions and planned follow-up meeting details.

### Notes and Observations  
Any additional noteworthy details not captured elsewhere.
---

# Action Items
- Define resource split and autonomy model for the clinic “factory” (SOPs, KPIs, Ops lead, escalation paths) — Owner: Oren + Shachar; Due: Dec 15, 2025
- Stand up Innovation/R&D stream with 10–20% budget and a clear charter (clinical data research, case-manager automation, app MVP, partnerships) — Owner: Dekel + Rami; Due: Dec 6, 2025
- Draft App MVP PRD (patient/clinician chat, proactive prompts, phone-based psychometrics, decision-support for psychiatrists) — Owner: Rami + Product; Due: Dec 12, 2025
- Launch case‑manager automation pilot in one clinic unit; success metrics agreed (cycle time, errors, satisfaction) — Owner: Rami + Eng; Due: Dec 20, 2025
- Prioritize data partnerships (universities, hospitals, pharma): target list + outreach template; propose governance/IRB plan — Owner: Dekel; Due: Dec 18, 2025
- Evaluate on‑device front-end transcription/automation partner (edge capture, secure sync, trigger words) — Owner: Oren + Rami; Due: Dec 11, 2025
- Define 2026 vision one‑pager and investor‑ready narrative (80/20 model, “learning system” moat, partnership thesis) — Owner: Rami + Oren; Due: Nov 29, 2025
- Clinic KPIs dashboard live (throughput, margins, NPS, SLA, outcomes) — Owner: Ops (Shachar); Due: Dec 5, 2025

# TL;DR
- Direction: Keep the clinic as the revenue engine but formalize it as an autonomous “factory,” while dedicating 10–20% of resources to an Innovation/R&D track that compounds a data/AI moat. As Oren put it, “אחרי זה אקזקיושן… ברגע שזה את הנישה… אתה נותן וליו” and Dekel emphasized “אנחנו בונים פה מערכת שהיא מערכת לומדת.”
- Product/AI: Ship an app MVP (patient/clinician chat, proactive data capture, phone-based psychometrics) and pilot case‑manager automation; add decision-support for psychiatrists.
- Data/Partnerships: Spin up clinical data research and external collaborations (universities, hospitals, pharma) toward insights, publications, and platform differentiation. Explore big-tech partnership readiness when data assets mature.
- Guardrails: Avoid getting trapped as service-only and avoid premature lateral moves; use the 10–20% innovation budget to explore adjacent plays without starving the core. As Rami noted, “אני לא הולך לחכות חמש שנים… יהיה 10–20%… ילך לחלומות.”

# Priorities & Challenges
- Priorities
  - Scale the clinic into a replicable, KPI-driven “factory” with minimal founder dependency.
  - Deliver automation (case-manager tasks), decision support, and an app MVP to accelerate data flywheel.
  - Stand up a rigorous clinical-data research program; convert unique datasets into insights and partnerships.
- Challenges
  - Focus discipline: tension between core growth vs cross-vertical exploration (“זה כמעט חברה בפני עצמה”).
  - Investor narrative: align on a clear, near-term vision while preserving long-term ambition.
  - Compliance and data governance for multi-party research/partnerships (IRB, consent, privacy, security).
  - Talent bandwidth: ensure ops autonomy so leadership can fuel R&D without operational drag.

# Open Questions
- What exact % allocation (10–20%) and gating criteria will govern the Innovation/R&D budget each quarter?
- When (and with what success metrics) should we enter psychotherapy and wellness lines?
- What is the partnership sequencing: pharma/universities first, or tech majors only after X data milestones?
- Who is the permanent Ops lead for the autonomous clinic unit, and what authority/KPIs will they own?
- What IP/data-governance framework (ownership, access tiers, de-identification) will we standardize for partners?

# Immediate Next Steps & Future Focus
- Next 2 weeks: finalize 80/20 model and clinic autonomy; kick off R&D charter; app PRD + automation pilot design; shortlist partners and prepare compliant outreach.
- 1–3 months: run case‑manager automation pilot; instrument phone-based psychometrics; publish first data insights; evaluate on‑device front-end integration; stand up KPI dashboard.
- Future focus: decision-support for psychiatrists, psychotherapy/wellness expansion readiness, big-tech partnership exploration, validation of real-time data methodology, insurance partnerships and international “factory” replication.
